  "findings": [
    "Primary and secondary connections are landed in reverse on the transformer terminals.",
    "Severe hazard of stepping up voltage to dangerous levels instead of stepping down, risking catastrophic equipment failure and arc flash."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Lock out and tag out the transformer immediately.",
    "Disconnect the reversed primary and secondary conductors.",
    "Re-terminate the conductors to their correct primary and secondary terminals per manufacturer submittals and schematics.",
    "Verify proper grounding and bonding per NEC 250.30 before re-testing."
  ],
  "rationale": "Landing the primary and secondary connections in reverse on a step-down transformer will step up the voltage dramatically when energized, creating an extreme safety hazard, violating NEC Art. 450, and risking immediate destruction of the equipment and upstream/downstream systems.",
  "references": [
    "NEC Art. 450",
    "NEC 250.30"
  ]
}
